root_text.an5.43:1.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an5.43:2.1: “Pañcime, gahapati, dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.43:2.3: Āyu, gahapati, iṭṭho kanto manāpo dullabho lokasmiṁ;
root_text.an5.43:2.4: vaṇṇo iṭṭho kanto manāpo dullabho lokasmiṁ;
root_text.an5.43:2.5: sukhaṁ iṭṭhaṁ kantaṁ manāpaṁ dullabhaṁ lokasmiṁ;
root_text.an5.43:2.6: yaso iṭṭho kanto manāpo dullabho lokasmiṁ;
root_text.an5.43:2.7: saggā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.43:2.8: Ime kho, gahapati, pañca dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā dullabhā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.43:3.1: Imesaṁ kho, gahapati, pañcannaṁ dhammānaṁ iṭṭhānaṁ kantānaṁ manāpānaṁ dullabhānaṁ lokasmiṁ na āyācanahetu vā patthanāhetu vā paṭilābhaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an5.43:3.2: Imesaṁ kho, gahapati, pañcannaṁ dhammānaṁ iṭṭhānaṁ kantānaṁ manāpānaṁ dullabhānaṁ lokasmiṁ āyācanahetu vā patthanāhetu vā paṭilābho abhavissa, ko idha kena hāyetha?
root_text.an5.43:4.1: Na kho, gahapati, arahati ariyasāvako āyukāmo āyuṁ āyācituṁ vā abhinandituṁ vā āyussa vāpi hetu.
root_text.an5.43:4.2: Āyukāmena, gahapati, ariyasāvakena āyusaṁvattanikā paṭipadā paṭipajjitabbā.
root_text.an5.43:4.3: Āyusaṁvattanikā hissa paṭipadā paṭipannā āyupaṭilābhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.43:4.4: So lābhī hoti āyussa dibbassa vā mānusassa vā.
root_text.an5.43:5.1: Na kho, gahapati, arahati ariyasāvako vaṇṇakāmo vaṇṇaṁ āyācituṁ vā abhinandituṁ vā vaṇṇassa vāpi hetu.
root_text.an5.43:5.2: Vaṇṇakāmena, gahapati, ariyasāvakena vaṇṇasaṁvattanikā paṭipadā paṭipajjitabbā.
root_text.an5.43:5.3: Vaṇṇasaṁvattanikā hissa paṭipadā paṭipannā vaṇṇapaṭilābhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.43:5.4: So lābhī hoti vaṇṇassa dibbassa vā mānusassa vā.
root_text.an5.43:6.1: Na kho, gahapati, arahati ariyasāvako sukhakāmo sukhaṁ āyācituṁ vā abhinandituṁ vā sukhassa vāpi hetu.
root_text.an5.43:6.2: Sukhakāmena, gahapati, ariyasāvakena sukhasaṁvattanikā paṭipadā paṭipajjitabbā.
root_text.an5.43:6.3: Sukhasaṁvattanikā hissa paṭipadā paṭipannā sukhapaṭilābhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.43:6.4: So lābhī hoti sukhassa dibbassa vā mānusassa vā.
root_text.an5.43:7.1: Na kho, gahapati, arahati ariyasāvako yasakāmo yasaṁ āyācituṁ vā abhinandituṁ vā yasassa vāpi hetu.
root_text.an5.43:7.2: Yasakāmena, gahapati, ariyasāvakena yasasaṁvattanikā paṭipadā paṭipajjitabbā.
root_text.an5.43:7.3: Yasasaṁvattanikā hissa paṭipadā paṭipannā yasapaṭilābhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an5.43:7.4: So lābhī hoti yasassa dibbassa vā mānusassa vā.
root_text.an5.43:8.1: Na kho, gahapati, arahati ariyasāvako saggakāmo saggaṁ āyācituṁ vā abhinandituṁ vā saggānaṁ vāpi hetu.
root_text.an5.43:8.2: Saggakāmena, gahapati, ariyasāvakena saggasaṁvattanikā paṭipadā paṭipajjitabbā.
root_text.an5.43:8.3: Saggasaṁvattanikā hissa paṭipadā paṭipannā saggapaṭilābhāya saṁvattati.

translation_text.an5.43:1.1: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an5.43:2.1: “Householder, these five things, which are likable, desirable, and agreeable are hard to get in the world.
translation_text.an5.43:2.8: These are the five things, which are likable, desirable, and agreeable, but hard to get in the world.
translation_text.an5.43:3.1: And I say that these five things are not got by supplication or wishing for them.
translation_text.an5.43:3.2: If they were, who would lack them?
translation_text.an5.43:4.1: A noble disciple who wants to live long ought not pray for it, or hope for it, or pine for it.
translation_text.an5.43:4.2: Instead, they should practice the way that leads to long life.
translation_text.an5.43:4.3: For by practicing that way they gain long life
translation_text.an5.43:4.4: as a god or a human being.
translation_text.an5.43:5.1: A noble disciple who wants to be beautiful ought not pray for it, or hope for it, or pine for it.
translation_text.an5.43:5.2: Instead, they should practice the way that leads to beauty.
translation_text.an5.43:5.3: For by practicing that way they gain beauty
translation_text.an5.43:5.4: as a god or a human being.
translation_text.an5.43:6.1: A noble disciple who wants to be happy ought not pray for it, or hope for it, or pine for it.
translation_text.an5.43:6.2: Instead, they should practice the way that leads to happiness.
translation_text.an5.43:6.3: For by practicing that way they gain happiness
translation_text.an5.43:6.4: as a god or a human being.
translation_text.an5.43:7.1: A noble disciple who wants to be famous ought not pray for it, or hope for it, or pine for it.
translation_text.an5.43:7.2: Instead, they should practice the way that leads to fame.
translation_text.an5.43:7.3: For by practicing that way they gain fame
translation_text.an5.43:7.4: as a god or a human being.
translation_text.an5.43:8.1: A noble disciple who wants to go to heaven ought not pray for it, or hope for it, or pine for it.
translation_text.an5.43:8.2: Instead, they should practice the way that leads to heaven.
translation_text.an5.43:8.3: For by practicing that way they gain heaven,
translation_text.an5.43:8.4: they are one who gains the heavens.
translation_text.an5.43:9.1: For one who desires lofty delights
translation_text.an5.43:9.3: long life, beauty, fame and reputation,
translation_text.an5.43:11.3: An attentive one, comprehending the meaning,

variant_text.an5.43:3.1: patthanāhetu vā → na patthanahetu vā (sya-all, km); na patthanāhetu vā (pts1ed)
